meta.name: BRCS Brief Resilient Coping Scale
meta.description: The BRCS is a 4-item scale designed to capture tendencies to cope with stress in a highly adaptive manner. It measures the ability to creatively and flexibly handle difficult situations, reflecting resilient coping as an active problem-solving approach to adversity.
meta.author: Sinclair & Wallston (2004)
meta.reference: Sinclair VG, Wallston KA. The development and psychometric evaluation of the Brief Resilient Coping Scale. Assessment. 2004;11(1):94-101.
meta.instruction: Please indicate the extent to which each of the following statements describes your behavior and actions.

commonChoices.label: Does not describe me at all

questions.text: I look for creative ways to alter difficult situations
Regardless of what happens to me, I believe I can control my reaction to it
I believe that I can grow in positive ways by dealing with difficult situations
I actively look for ways to replace the losses I encounter in life

ranges.label: Low Resilience
ranges.description: You have low resilient coping. You may have difficulty finding adaptive ways to deal with stressful situations.
suggestions: Try to identify at least one positive aspect in each challenge you face
Build a network of supportive relationships
Practice problem-solving by breaking large problems into smaller steps
Consider speaking with a counselor about developing coping strategies

ranges.label: Moderate Resilience
ranges.description: You have moderate resilient coping abilities. You sometimes find creative solutions but may struggle with particularly challenging situations.
suggestions: Practice reframing negative situations in a more balanced way
Engage in activities that build confidence and mastery
Reflect on past difficulties you have successfully overcome

ranges.label: Moderately High Resilience
ranges.description: You have moderately high resilient coping. You generally approach difficulties with adaptive strategies and can often find creative solutions.
suggestions: Continue applying your flexible approach to challenges
Share your coping strategies with others who may benefit
Keep building on your strengths while acknowledging areas for growth
Maintain practices that support your resilience

ranges.label: High Resilience
ranges.description: You have high resilient coping. You consistently approach adversity with creativity, flexibility, and active problem-solving.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your strong resilient coping style
Your approach to difficulties can inspire and help others
Remember to balance active coping with self-care
Stay open to learning new strategies even when current ones work well
